title: cynanchica setulosa
intro: Cynanchica setulosa, commonly known as the "Yellow-stemmed Milkvine", is a plant species found in the tropical regions of Asia. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can aid in wound healing, fever reduction, and the treatment of respiratory issues. The therapeutic actions of Cynanchica setulosa are attributed to its bioactive constituents, such as alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which exhibit immunomodulatory and antioxidant effects. Further research is necessary to confirm the efficacy of Cynanchica setulosa in treating various health conditions.
benefits: Cynanchica setulosa has many benefits, such as its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reducing the risk of heart disease and promoting overall cardiovascular health. This plant also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, gout, and other inflammatory conditions, improving joint mobility and reducing pain. Furthermore, cynanchica setulosa has been shown to have antioxidant effects, protecting against cell damage and potentially reducing the risk of certain cancers. By incorporating cynanchica setulosa into one's lifestyle, individuals may experience improved cognitive function, enhanced immune system, and increased energy levels, ultimately leading to a better quality of life and improved overall well-being.
actions: Cynanchica setulosa has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, antioxidant, and anti-cancer properties, making it a valuable resource in the treatment of various health conditions. The plant's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate pain and reduce swelling, improving the quality of life for individuals suffering from arthritis, gout, and other inflammatory diseases. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of cynanchica setulosa help protect against oxidative stress, which can contribute to age-related diseases such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's, allowing individuals to maintain their cognitive function and independence as they age. By reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and protecting against chronic diseases, cynanchica setulosa has the potential to improve daily life by increasing mobility, reducing medication usage, and enhancing overall well-being.
intro_study: According to "Biomedicine & pharmacotherapy = Biomedecine & pharmacotherapie", cynanchica setulosa benefits are derived from its flavonoid cynaroside, which exhibits various health benefits such as antibacterial, antifungal, antioxidant, and anticancer effects.
constituents: Cynanchica setulosa has many active constituents, such as alkaloids (vincristine and vinblastine), glycosides (cynanchoside and setuloside), and flavonoids (kaempferol and quercetin), which exhibit a range of therapeutic properties. These compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can improve people's daily lives by reducing pain and inflammation, preventing infections, and protecting against oxidative stress. The alkaloids present in Cynanchica setulosa have been found to be effective in treating various types of cancer, including leukemia and lymphoma, by inhibiting cell proliferation and inducing apoptosis. Additionally, the flavonoids present in the plant have been shown to have neuroprotective effects, which can help to prevent or slow down the progression of neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's.
parts: Cynanchica setulosa has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, leaves, and flowers, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The roots of Cynanchica setulosa are known to contain glycosides, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral activities. The leaves of the plant, on the other hand, contain alkaloids and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-cancer properties. The flowers of Cynanchica setulosa contain flavonoids and saponins, which have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities.
preparations: Cynanchica setulosa has many herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions. A tea made from the leaves of the Cynanchica setulosa plant is used to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, such as colds and flu. A tincture of the plant is used to treat digestive issues, including diarrhea and nausea, due to its anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, a topical application of the plant's extract can be used to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
side_effects: Cynanchica setulosa can have side effects if used improperly, such as severe gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Overuse of the plant can also lead to cardiovascular problems, including increased heart rate and blood pressure. Additionally, it may cause allergic reactions, including skin irritation, itching, and respiratory issues like wheezing and shortness of breath. In severe cases, improper use of cynanchica setulosa can lead to life-threatening complications, such as kidney or liver damage.
